---
title: "Clustbench Performance Analysis"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
```

## Load Data

```{r load-data}
# Run the Python script and capture JSON output
json_output <- system("python3 parse_results.py 2>/dev/null", intern = TRUE)
json_text <- paste(json_output, collapse = "\n")

# Parse JSON
results_list <- fromJSON(json_text, flatten = FALSE)

# Convert to data frame
df <- results_list %>%
  # Unnest performance metrics into columns (already numeric from Python)
  mutate(
    # Extract performance metrics
    runtime_s = performance$s,
    max_rss = performance$max_rss,
    max_vms = performance$max_vms,
    max_uss = performance$max_uss,
    max_pss = performance$max_pss,
    io_in = performance$io_in,
    io_out = performance$io_out,
    mean_load = performance$mean_load,
    cpu_time = performance$cpu_time
  ) %>%
  # Select relevant columns
  select(backend, rep, generator, dataset_name, method, method_params, method_full,
         runtime_s, max_rss, max_vms, max_uss, max_pss, 
         io_in, io_out, mean_load, cpu_time, metrics)

# Display summary
glimpse(df)
head(df)
```

## Summary Statistics

```{r summary}
summary(df %>% select(runtime_s, max_rss, cpu_time, mean_load))
```

## Metrics Analysis

```{r extract-metrics}
# Helper function to safely extract metric value
get_metric_value <- function(metrics, family, metric_name, k) {
  tryCatch({
    if (is.null(metrics)) return(NA)
    if (is.null(metrics[[family]])) return(NA)
    if (is.null(metrics[[family]][[metric_name]])) return(NA)
    
    # Check for error field
    metric_data <- metrics[[family]][[metric_name]]
    if (!is.null(metric_data$error)) return(NA)
    
    # Get value for k
    value <- metric_data[[as.character(k)]]
    if (is.null(value)) return(NA)
    return(as.numeric(value))
  }, error = function(e) {
    return(NA)
  })
}

# Extract all unique metric families and names from the data
all_families <- unique(unlist(lapply(results_list$metrics, names)))
all_metrics <- list()
for (fam in all_families) {
  if (!is.null(fam)) {
    metrics_in_family <- unique(unlist(lapply(results_list$metrics, function(m) {
      if (!is.null(m[[fam]])) names(m[[fam]]) else NULL
    })))
    all_metrics[[fam]] <- metrics_in_family[!is.null(metrics_in_family)]
  }
}

# Create metrics dataframe with same columns as performance df
metrics_df <- results_list %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract partition metrics for k=2, k=3, k=4
    nca_k2 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "normalized_clustering_accuracy", 2),
    nca_k3 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "normalized_clustering_accuracy", 3),
    nca_k4 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "normalized_clustering_accuracy", 4),
    afm_k2 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "adjusted_fm_score", 2),
    afm_k3 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "adjusted_fm_score", 3),
    afm_k4 = get_metric_value(metrics, "partition_metrics", "adjusted_fm_score", 4)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(backend, rep, generator, dataset_name, method, method_params, method_full,
         nca_k2, nca_k3, nca_k4, afm_k2, afm_k3, afm_k4)

head(metrics_df)
glimpse(metrics_df)

# Summary of metrics by method
metrics_df %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    mean_nca_k2 = mean(nca_k2, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_afm_k2 = mean(afm_k2, na.rm = TRUE),
    na_count = sum(is.na(nca_k2))
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_nca_k2))
```

### Metrics by Method

```{r plot-metrics-method, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
metrics_long <- metrics_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(nca_k2, nca_k3, nca_k4, afm_k2, afm_k3, afm_k4),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  separate(metric, into = c("metric_type", "k"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(
    metric_type = case_when(
      metric_type == "nca" ~ "Normalized Clustering Accuracy",
      metric_type == "afm" ~ "Adjusted FM Score",
      TRUE ~ metric_type
    )
  )

ggplot(metrics_long %>% filter(!is.na(score)), 
       aes(x = method, y = score, fill = k)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~metric_type, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Clustering Metrics by Method",
    x = "Method",
    y = "Score",
    fill = "K"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### Runtime vs Accuracy Trade-off

```{r plot-runtime-accuracy, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(metrics_df %>% filter(!is.na(nca_k2)), 
       aes(x = runtime_s, y = nca_k2, color = method, shape = backend)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Runtime vs Clustering Accuracy Trade-off (k=2)",
    x = "Runtime (seconds)",
    y = "Normalized Clustering Accuracy",
    color = "Method",
    shape = "Backend"
  )
```

## Performance by Method

```{r performance-by-method}
df %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    mean_runtime = mean(runtime_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_runtime = median(runtime_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_memory = mean(max_rss, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_cpu = mean(cpu_time, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_runtime)
```

## Visualizations

### Runtime by Method

```{r plot-runtime-method, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = method, y = runtime_s, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~backend) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Runtime by Method and Backend",
    x = "Method",
    y = "Runtime (seconds)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### Memory Usage by Method

```{r plot-memory-method, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = method, y = max_rss, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~backend) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Memory Usage (RSS) by Method and Backend",
    x = "Method",
    y = "Max RSS (MB)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### Runtime vs Memory

```{r plot-runtime-memory, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = runtime_s, y = max_rss, color = method, shape = backend)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Runtime vs Memory Usage",
    x = "Runtime (seconds)",
    y = "Max RSS (MB)",
    color = "Method",
    shape = "Backend"
  )
```

### CPU Time vs Runtime

```{r plot-cpu-runtime, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = runtime_s, y = cpu_time, color = method)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  facet_wrap(~backend) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "CPU Time vs Runtime",
    x = "Runtime (seconds)",
    y = "CPU Time (seconds)",
    color = "Method",
    caption = "Dashed line shows 1:1 ratio"
  )
```

### Mean Load by Method

```{r plot-load-method, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = method, y = mean_load, fill = backend)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean CPU Load by Method",
    x = "Method",
    y = "Mean Load (%)",
    fill = "Backend"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### Performance by Dataset

```{r plot-runtime-dataset, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = dataset_name, y = runtime_s, fill = method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~backend) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Runtime by Dataset and Method",
    x = "Dataset",
    y = "Runtime (seconds)",
    fill = "Method"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Backend Comparison

```{r backend-comparison}
df %>%
  group_by(backend, method) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_runtime = mean(runtime_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_memory = mean(max_rss, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = backend,
    values_from = c(mean_runtime, mean_memory)
  )
```

## Export Data

```{r export}
# Save as CSV for further analysis
write_csv(df, "clustbench_performance.csv")
```
